lines: The rope hangs slack in your hand.
The chapel bell has already spent its warning.
The repaired rope runs through the scored pulley and into the bell crown above you.
Ringing it will tell the Remnant that Ash Chapel is safe enough to enter and that the families can leave the dark.
This ends the Chapel tutorial and moves you onto the Hallowfen road. Are you sure?

effects.log: You haul the bell rope. Iron answers over Ash Chapel.

questUpdate.log: Quest updated: the chapel bell has rung. The Hallowfen checkpoints still have not answered.

showBriefing.title: ACT I: THE HALLOWFEN
pages: The rope bites into your hands. Above the nave, old iron wakes after years of dust.
The sound rolls through the bell room, down the wall stair, across the broken garden, and into every room where someone is trying not to listen.
The Remnant will know the chapel is safe enough to enter. Your writ is not finished.
You broke one cell, not the hand that sent it here.
The Hallowfen checkpoints stopped answering before this cult reached the house. The wall sent neither bell nor runner, and no innocent mistake could have silenced both.
Follow the Hallowfen road. Find who silenced the towers.

page: The ledger gave the cell its name: the Choir of the Open Wound.
A Gate Mother's road order put Hallowfen in their path before the chapel went quiet. Whoever writes those orders knew the wall bells had stopped.
Below, Susanna starts counting people by name instead of by hiding place. The broom in her hands sounds too small for the work, and still she keeps at it.
Tobias stays beside the frame until the last vibration leaves the iron. He gives you one short nod toward the Hallowfen road.
Micah keeps both arms around the blue ball, as if color itself might need guarding.
Ruth watches from the chalk marks and tries to look bored instead of relieved.
Priscilla's tin saint rides in your pack, warm from her hand.
Jeremiah went toward Hallowfen for wall pay. If he is alive, the road has him.
The cross behind the altar no longer whispers.
Mercy made no speech when it left him, only weight on the stone and a silence that did not feel clean.
In the cellar, the bound guard does not have to learn the cult's next word.
The post stands empty in the cold.

lines: If it rings before the last cultists are dead, it calls people into a chapel that still has knives in it.
The rope pulls against a dead weight.
The bell frame is still sabotaged. Tobias Porta can set the missing pin once you bring him to the frame.
The rope pulls against a dead weight.
The bell frame is still sabotaged. Someone who knows old frames could set the missing pin.
